summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a sample space in probability?

Probability = (favorable outcomes) / (total outcomes). Working through the calculation gives The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment. The other options — 'The most likely outcome of an experiment', 'A single result from an experiment', and 'A subset of outcomes that we are interested in' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is an event in probability?

Probability = (favorable outcomes) / (total outcomes). Working through the calculation gives A subset of the sample space. The other options — 'The entire sample space', 'A single trial of an experiment', and 'The number of times an experiment is repeated' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which tool is best for displaying the sample space of a two-stage experiment with different options at each stage?

Tree diagram — branch for each choice; total outcomes = product of branch counts. A sample space is the set of all possible outcomes. The other options — 'Bar graph', 'Pie chart', and 'Venn diagram' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
